page: Amazon Web Services m8id.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. Memory capacity stands at 192.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 7 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 2850 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance across memory, compression, and compute tasks. The instance achieved top-tier results in Geekbench single-core (2215.0), image processing (20436.0), and text processing (2863.0). Memory bandwidth tests showed strong performance, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 3830.37 GB/sec and large block uncached speeds at 195.99 GB/sec. Multi-threaded decompression via bzip3 registered a strong score of 340.25 GB/sec. For database operations, the instance demonstrated strong performance with a Passmark database score of 24117.07 and Redis SET operations reaching 86.64 million operations per second.

The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations, which accelerate machine learning and cryptographic tasks. This is reflected in strong LLM inference speeds, processing 251.06 tokens per second for prompt processing on a medium 7B model. Given its balanced CPU-to-memory ratio, high-speed local NVMe storage, and strong cryptographic and database benchmark results, the m8id.12xlarge is suited for database hosting, static web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale language model inference workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers strong performance across multiple benchmarks, achieving top-tier ratings in Geekbench single-core, image processing, and text processing. It also demonstrates strong memory bandwidth, with read-only cached speeds of 3830.37 GB/sec, and strong database capabilities, processing 86.64 million Redis SET operations per second. Supported by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, this instance is highly suited for database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 2850 GB NVMe SSD local storage, delivering strong database and compute performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.12xlarge features 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 2850 GB NVMe SSD storage for general-purpose and database workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring high-speed DDR5 memory, integrated local NVMe SSD storage, and advanced Intel Xeon compute capabilities.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor
192.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core, image processing, and text processing benchmark performance
Strong database capabilities with 86.64 million Redis SET operations per second
Hardware-accelerated execution via Intel AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
Well-suited for database hosting, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference
